title: Getting started with ustats
text: users need: first call, reticulate (>= 1.41) automatically downloads private Python together required packages (u-stats, numpy, torch) cached environment, reuses later sessions.
code: install.packages("ustats") library(ustats)  H <- matrix(rnorm(100), 10, 10) ustat(list(H, H), "ab,bc->")

what: How the Python environment is resolved
title: Getting started with ustats
text: ustats declares Python dependencies via reticulate::py_require() package loaded. Python first needed, reticulate resolves requirements follows: already configured Python environment — via reticulate::use_virtualenv(), reticulate::use_condaenv(), RETICULATE_PYTHON environment variable — environment used. must contain u-stats, numpy, (recommended) torch. Otherwise, reticulate provisions ephemeral, cached environment containing declared packages automatically. Nothing installed system Python. therefore three ways set things , least manual.

text: nothing. first call touches Python triggers automatic setup: Two things know: first call downloads Python packages ; later calls later R sessions reuse cache. Linux, default PyTorch build PyPI bundles CUDA libraries (roughly 2.5 GB). NVIDIA GPU prefer small CPU-build (roughly 200 MB), use Option 2 — , Python initializes, replace declared requirement CPU-one.
code: library(ustats) ustat(list(matrix(rnorm(100), 10, 10)), "ab->")

what: Option 2: a persistent environment with setup_ustats()
title: Getting started with ustats
text: setup_ustats() creates dedicated environment installs dependencies . default installs CPU-PyTorch build: GPU builds Windows, wheel matching specific CUDA version, see https://pytorch.org/get-started/locally/ use Option 3.
code: library(ustats)  setup_ustats()                # virtualenv/conda + CPU-only torch setup_ustats(gpu = TRUE)      # default PyPI torch (CUDA-enabled on Linux) setup_ustats(   method  = "virtualenv",     # or "conda"   envname = "r-ustats",   persist = TRUE              # print the RETICULATE_PYTHON line to add )                             # to your .Rprofile (no files are written)

title: Getting started with ustats
text: already maintain conda virtualenv environment (example, one carefully chosen CUDA-enabled PyTorch), install one missing piece: tell reticulate use environment Python initializes (.e. right loading package, first ustat() call): Alternatively, set RETICULATE_PYTHON path Python binary .Renviron .Rprofile, takes effect sessions.
code: pip install u-stats library(ustats) reticulate::use_condaenv("your_env_name", required = TRUE) # or: reticulate::use_virtualenv("~/.virtualenvs/your_env")

title: Getting started with ustats
code: check_ustats_setup() #> === ustats Environment Status === #> #> [OK] Python: /path/to/python #>   Version: 3.12 #> [OK] u_stats available #> [OK] NumPy available #> [OK] PyTorch available (version 2.5.1, CUDA available) #> #> --------------------------------- #>  Environment fully ready (Torch backend available)

title: Getting started with ustats
text: ustat() takes list kernel tensors (R vectors matrices) Einstein summation expression describing indices contracted, distinct letters ranging distinct observation indices: index structure can equivalently given list numeric index vectors, convenient expression built programmatically:
code: library(ustats)  set.seed(1) n <- 300  H1 <- rnorm(n) H2 <- matrix(rnorm(n * n), n, n) H3 <- rnorm(n)  result <- ustat(   tensors    = list(H1, H2, H2, H3),   expression = "a,ab,bc,c->",   backend    = "torch",  # falls back to numpy if torch is unavailable   average    = TRUE,     # divide by the number of index tuples   dtype      = NULL      # auto: float32 on GPU, float64 on CPU ) print(result) ustat(list(H1, H2, H2, H3), list(1, c(1, 2), c(2, 3), 3))

title: Getting started with ustats
text: backend = "torch", computations run GPU automatically whenever PyTorch reports CUDA available:
code: torch <- reticulate::import("torch") torch$cuda$is_available()

title: Getting started with ustats
text: check_ustats_setup() reports missing module. session bound Python environment lacks dependency. Either install (pip install u-stats), restart R select different environment (Options 2-3 ). reticulate ignores use_condaenv() / RETICULATE_PYTHON. reticulate binds single Python per R session, moment Python first initialized. Restart R configure environment anything touches Python. first call seems stuck. likely downloading PyTorch; see Options 1-2 choose much smaller CPU-build. ustat() warns “Torch backend available”. bound environment PyTorch; computation falls back NumPy, slower can less numerically stable. Install torch setup_ustats() pip install torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu.

Chen X, Zhang R, Liu L (2025). ustats: R Interface Python Tools Computing Higher-Order U-Statistics. R package version 0.1.5, https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=ustats.

what: R Interface to Python Tools for Computing Higher-Order U-Statistics
title: R Interface to Python Tools for Computing Higher-Order U-Statistics
text: ustats provides R interface Python package u-stats computing higher-order U-statistics efficiently. Heavy numerical computation handled Python (via numpy.einsum torch.einsum), R serves convenient front-end data handling statistical workflows.

title: R Interface to Python Tools for Computing Higher-Order U-Statistics
text: package accompanies paper: Xingyu Chen, Ruiqi Zhang, Lin Liu. computing complexity computing higher-order U-statistics, exactly. arXiv preprint arXiv:2508.12627, 2025. underlying Python implementation PyPI package u-stats (source code: U-Statistics-python). R package lightweight interface built reticulate.

title: R Interface to Python Tools for Computing Higher-Order U-Statistics
text: package needs Python (>= 3.11) Python packages u-stats, numpy, torch. three ways get — pick one fits :
code: # From CRAN: install.packages("ustats")  # Development version from GitHub: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("cxy0714/U-Statistics-R")

title: R Interface to Python Tools for Computing Higher-Order U-Statistics
text: reticulate (>= 1.41), Python dependencies declared package provisioned automatically first time Python needed. Just call ustat(): downloaded environment cached reused across sessions. Note: first call downloads PyTorch. Linux default build bundles CUDA libraries (~2.5 GB); prefer small CPU-build want full control, use Option 2 3.
code: library(ustats)  H <- matrix(rnorm(100), 10, 10) ustat(list(H, H), "ab,bc->")   # first call sets up Python automatically

title: R Interface to Python Tools for Computing Higher-Order U-Statistics
text: Creates persistent environment installs dependencies. default installs CPU-build PyTorch (~200 MB instead ~2.5 GB):
code: library(ustats) setup_ustats()                                     # CPU-only PyTorch (default) setup_ustats(gpu = TRUE)                           # default PyPI PyTorch (CUDA on Linux) setup_ustats(method = "virtualenv", envname = "r-ustats")

what: Option 3 — Use your own Python/conda environment
title: R Interface to Python Tools for Computing Higher-Order U-Statistics
text: already environment configured PyTorch (e.g. specific CUDA version), just add u-stats: point reticulate environment Python initializes:
code: pip install u-stats library(ustats) reticulate::use_condaenv("your_env_name", required = TRUE)  # or use_virtualenv() # Alternatively, set the RETICULATE_PYTHON environment variable # (e.g. in .Rprofile or .Renviron) to the path of your python binary.

title: R Interface to Python Tools for Computing Higher-Order U-Statistics
text: Whichever option chose: See vignette("ustats") complete installation guide troubleshooting tips.
code: check_ustats_setup() #> === ustats Environment Status === #> [OK] Python: /path/to/python #> [OK] u_stats available #> [OK] NumPy available #> [OK] PyTorch available (version 2.x, CUDA available)

title: R Interface to Python Tools for Computing Higher-Order U-Statistics
text: structure can also given index list instead string:
code: library(ustats)  set.seed(1) n <- 300  H1 <- rnorm(n) H2 <- matrix(rnorm(n * n), n, n) H3 <- rnorm(n)  # U-statistic defined by the Einstein summation "a,ab,bc,c->" result <- ustat(   tensors    = list(H1, H2, H2, H3),   expression = "a,ab,bc,c->",   backend    = "torch",  # falls back to numpy if torch is unavailable   dtype      = NULL      # auto: float32 on GPU, float64 on CPU ) print(result) ustat(list(H1, H2, H2, H3), list(1, c(1, 2), c(2, 3), 3))

title: R Interface to Python Tools for Computing Higher-Order U-Statistics
text: PyTorch detects CUDA GPU, ustat(..., backend = "torch") uses automatically: get CUDA-enabled PyTorch, run setup_ustats(gpu = TRUE) (Linux), install wheel matching CUDA version pytorch.org environment reticulate uses (Option 3 ).
code: torch <- reticulate::import("torch") torch$cuda$is_available()

what: Set Up Python Environment for ustats — setup_ustats
title: Set Up Python Environment for ustats — setup_ustats
text: Installs configures Python environment required run ustat(), including u_stats, numpy, torch.

title: Set Up Python Environment for ustats — setup_ustats
code: setup_ustats(   method = c("auto", "virtualenv", "conda", "system"),   envname = "r-ustats",   gpu = FALSE,   restart = FALSE,   persist = FALSE )

title: Set Up Python Environment for ustats — setup_ustats
text: method Installation method Python: "auto" (default): use existing Python install Miniconda "virtualenv": create virtual environment "conda": create conda environment "system": use system Python envname Name virtualenv/conda environment (default: "r-ustats") gpu Logical; FALSE (default), install CPU-build PyTorch official PyTorch wheel index (https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu). CPU build much smaller (roughly 200 MB instead 2 GB bundled CUDA libraries Linux) sufficient machines without NVIDIA GPU. Set gpu = TRUE install default PyPI build PyTorch, includes CUDA support Linux; GPU builds Windows, specific CUDA version, see https://pytorch.org/get-started/locally/. restart Logical; whether restart R session setup persist Logical; TRUE, print RETICULATE_PYTHON configuration line can add .Rprofile make environment persist across sessions. function never writes files (default: FALSE)

title: Set Up Python Environment for ustats — setup_ustats
text: Invisibly returns TRUE setup completed environment verifies, FALSE otherwise.

title: Set Up Python Environment for ustats — setup_ustats
text: users need call function. reticulate (>= 1.41), Python dependencies declared package provisioned automatically cached environment first time Python used (e.g. first call ustat()). Call setup_ustats() prefer persistent, dedicated environment, want control PyTorch installed (see gpu argument). Note: PyTorch strongly recommended. NumPy backend slower may numerically less stable higher-order U-statistics.

title: Set Up Python Environment for ustats — setup_ustats
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ setup_ustats()                # CPU-only PyTorch (small, default) setup_ustats(gpu = TRUE)      # default PyPI PyTorch (CUDA on Linux) setup_ustats(method = "conda", envname = "ustats-env") } # }

what: Compute a Higher-Order U-Statistic via Python — ustat
title: Compute a Higher-Order U-Statistic via Python — ustat
text: Computes higher-order U-statistic precomputed kernel tensors using Python package u_stats. function serves R interface handles automatic data conversion via reticulate.

title: Compute a Higher-Order U-Statistic via Python — ustat
code: ustat(   tensors,   expression,   backend = c("torch", "numpy"),   average = TRUE,   dtype = NULL )

title: Compute a Higher-Order U-Statistic via Python — ustat
text: tensors list numeric vectors, matrices, arrays representing kernel evaluations. tensors must compatible dimensions. expression Either character string Einstein notation list numeric vectors length 1 2 describing index structure. backend Character string specifying computation backend: "torch" (default) "numpy". average Logical; TRUE (default), return averaged U-statistic. Otherwise returns raw sum. dtype Optional character string specifying numeric precision tensors converted R. Must one "float32" "float64". NULL (default), precision chosen automatically: float32 using Torch backend CUDA available float64 otherwise

title: Compute a Higher-Order U-Statistic via Python — ustat
text: numeric scalar containing computed U-statistic.

title: Compute a Higher-Order U-Statistic via Python — ustat
text: U-statistic structure can specified using either: Einstein summation string (e.g. "ab,bc->"), nested list index vectors (e.g. list(c(1,2), c(2,3))) function requires working Python environment u_stats package installed. reticulate (>= 1.41) required Python packages provisioned automatically first time Python used, manual setup needed cases. create persistent environment instead (choose CPU-CUDA builds PyTorch), use setup_ustats(); use check_ustats_setup() verify configuration. R numeric objects converted NumPy arrays using selected precision. Python tensors (e.g., Torch tensors) supplied directly, passed unchanged.

title: Compute a Higher-Order U-Statistic via Python — ustat
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ setup_ustats()  v1 <- runif(100) H1 <- matrix(runif(100), 10, 10) H2 <- matrix(runif(100), 10, 10)  ustat(list(H1, H2), "ab,bc->") ustat(list(H1, H2), "ab,bc->", dtype = "float32") ustat(list(H1, H2), "ab,bc->", dtype = NULL)  # auto precision } # }

what: ustats: R Interface to Python Tools for Computing Higher-Order U-Statistics — ustats-package
title: ustats: R Interface to Python Tools for Computing Higher-Order U-Statistics — ustats-package
text: Provides R interface Python package 'u-stats' https://pypi.org/project/u-stats/ efficient computation higher-order U-statistics using Einstein summation notation, implementing methods Chen, Zhang, Liu (2025) doi:10.48550/arXiv.2508.12627 . package automatically converts R objects 'NumPy' 'PyTorch' tensors via 'reticulate' supports GPU acceleration 'PyTorch' 'CUDA' available. Python dependencies declared via 'reticulate' can installed automatically first use. Designed large-scale statistical estimation numerical stability performance critical.

title: ustats (development version)
text: Added CITATION file: citation("ustats") now returns package reference paper describing implemented methods (Chen, Zhang, Liu, 2025, doi:10.48550/arXiv.2508.12627). Added package-level help page, accessible via ?ustats package?ustats, summarising package linking documentation site.

title: ustats 0.1.5
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-18 DESCRIPTION now cites paper describing implemented methods: Chen, Zhang, Liu (2025) doi:10.48550/arXiv.2508.12627. setup_ustats(persist = TRUE) longer offers write project .Rprofile. now prints RETICULATE_PYTHON line user add manually, package never writes user’s file space (CRAN policy).

title: ustats 0.1.4
text: Python dependencies (u-stats, numpy, torch) now declared reticulate::py_require() .onLoad(). reticulate (>= 1.41) provisioned automatically cached environment first use, users need manual setup . reticulate (>= 1.41) now required. setup_ustats() gains gpu argument. default (gpu = FALSE) much smaller CPU-PyTorch build installed official PyTorch wheel index; gpu = TRUE installs default PyPI build (CUDA-enabled Linux). check_ustats_setup() now reports detected PyTorch version whether CUDA available, prints actionable installation hints something missing. Added “Getting started” vignette complete installation guide (automatic setup, setup_ustats(), bring---environment) troubleshooting tips; README installation section rewritten accordingly.

title: ustats 0.1.4
text: setup_ustats() now invisibly returns TRUE/FALSE documented (previously returned NULL persistence step unreachable dead code placed return value). Fixed duplicated “Description:” prefix DESCRIPTION file.

title: ustats 0.1.4
text: Python-dependent tests now additionally guarded skip_on_cran(), test suite never initializes Python (triggers automatic dependency downloads) CRAN machines. expr_list_to_einstein() internal longer generates help page.

title: ustats 0.1.3
text: Fixed NEWS.md filename casing (News.md -> NEWS.md).

title: ustats 0.1.2
text: Quoted software names (‘NumPy’, ‘PyTorch’, ‘CUDA’) DESCRIPTION per CRAN policy.

title: ustats 0.1.1
text: Minor improvements CRAN compliance fixes. Fixed issues identified CRAN pre-checks, including: Proper handling external dependencies Removal non-ASCII characters Namespace import adjustments Compliance CRAN policies user system modifications

title: ustats 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release.
